zh.lifeSummary: 當John Howard於大約1310年出生在英國素福爾可時，他的父親John Howard36歲，母親Joan Cornwall27歲。他於1334年與Alice de Bois在英國英格蘭波克夏瓦林福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他在英國諾福爾可被列為sheriff of norfolk。他去世於1364年4月29日之前在英國薩默塞特巴斯，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Howard于大约1310年出生在英国素福爾可时，他的父亲John Howard36岁，母亲Joan Cornwall27岁。他于1334年与Alice de Bois在英国英格兰波克夏瓦林福德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他在英国諾福爾可被列为sheriff of norfolk。他去世于1364年4月29日之前在英国薩默塞特巴斯，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可。

nameMeanings.name: Howard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal names Huward (also Howard) and Heward, from Old French Huard (itself from ancient Germanic Hugihard, hugi- ‘mind, understanding, spirit’ + hard- ‘hardy, bold’). As Hugh appears in Middle English as both How and Hew, this is the definite origin of Heward and a source of Howard. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Hugh .

English: from the Middle English personal name Haward or Howard, usually an Anglicized form of Old Danish Hāwarth (Old Norse Hávarthr, from há ‘high’ + varthr ‘guard, guardian, warden’). Alternation between Haward and Howard may have led to later confusion with Hayward .
English: occasionally a variant of   Ewart   2.

variants.name: Fogarty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, James Patrick, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fógartaigh ‘son of Fógartach’, a personal name from fógartha ‘proclaimed, banished, outlawed’. It is sometimes Anglicized as Howard .

variants.name: Huggard
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the medieval personal name Hugard, a variant of Huard (see Howard ). This surname is also established in Ireland (Kerry).

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.

variants.name: Huard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Adelard, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Marcel, Alcide, Amiee, Armand, Aurele, Clermont, Fernard.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the Old French personal name Huart, either identical in origin with Howard 1 or a pet form of Old French Hue .
History: Jean Huard from Courson-Monteloup in Essonne, France, married Anne-Marie Amiot in Quebec City, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John Howard was born about 1310, in Suffolk, England, his father, John Howard, was 36 and his mother, Joan Cornwall, was 27. He married Alice de Bois in 1334, in Wallingford, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. His occupation is listed as sheriff of norfolk in Norfolk, England. He died before 29 April 1364, in Bath, Somerset, England, and was buried in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Howard est né environ 1310, à Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, John Howard, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Joan Cornwall, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Bois en 1334, à Wallingford, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of norfolk à Norfolk, Angleterre. Il est décédé avant 29 avril 1364, à Bath, Somerset, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als sheriff of norfolk in Norfolk, England aufgeführt. Er starb vor 29. Lui sposato Alice de Bois nel 1334, nel Wallingford, Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of norfolk nel Norfolk, England. Morì prima del 29 aprile 1364, nel Bath, Somerset, England, e fu sepolto a Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: John Howard(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Howard(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Cornwall(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1334년 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 월링퍼드에서 Alice de Bois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 노르폴크에서 sheriff of norfolk이었다. 그는 1364년 4월 29일 이전 영국 서머세트 바스에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Howard nasceu aproximadamente 1310, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Howard, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Joan Cornwall, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Bois em 1334, em Wallingford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como sheriff of norfolk em Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu antes 29 de abril de 1364, em Bath, Somerset, Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Howard родился около 1310 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени John Howard было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Cornwall, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Bois в 1334 г. в местности валлингфорд, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии sheriff of norfolk в местности норфолк, Англия. Он умер перед 29 апрель 1364 г. в местности бат, сомирсет, Англия и был похоронен в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Howard nació aproximadamente 1310, en Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Howard, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Joan Cornwall, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Alice de Bois en 1334, en Wallingford, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era sheriff of norfolk en Norfolk, Inglaterra. Murió antes de 29 de abril de 1364, en Bath, Somerset, Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
